title: Why Moltbook Matters

mentions.context_snippet: Multbook launched around Wednesday, and by Friday morning had something like 2,000 agents that were interacting on the site...

facts.fact_value: A social network platform for AI agents interacting with each other.

mentions.context_snippet: people started playing around with a new assistant platform called ClaudeBot, that was C-L-A-W-D...

facts.fact_value: An assistant platform allowing personal agent operation with access to user life parts.

mentions.context_snippet: ClaudeBot due to copyright concerns from Anthropic changed their name first to Multi and then finally to OpenClaw...

facts.fact_value: Renamed from ClaudeBot due to copyright concerns

mentions.quoted_text: there is a new kind of coding I call vibe-coding
mentions.context_snippet: one year ago, 365 days, that Andrej Karpathy tweeted there is a new kind of coding I call vibe-coding...

facts.fact_value: Introduced the term 'vibe-coding' one year ago

mentions.quoted_text: Why OpenClaw Feels Alive Even Though It's Not
mentions.context_snippet: IAI host Claire Vo wrote a post about this called Why OpenClaw Feels Alive Even Though It's Not...

facts.fact_value: Scheduled agent that runs every 30 minutes to do proactive work
Allows agents to send messages to each other enqueuing work

mentions.quoted_text: Multbook is nothing more than a puppeted multi-agent LLM loop. Each quote-unquote agent is just next-token prediction shaped by human-defined prompts...
mentions.context_snippet: xy.dot writes, Multbook is nothing more than a puppeted multi-agent LLM loop...

mentions.quoted_text: The many eulogies for AI capability growth after the release of GPT-5 seem especially short-sighted right now.
mentions.context_snippet: Ethan Mollack wrote, The many eulogies for AI capability growth after the release of GPT-5 seem especially short-sighted right now.
You'll want to check out another podcast that I've had the privilege to host, which is called You Can With AI from KPMG...
mentions.review_reason: Podcast is not in locked taxonomy; marked needs_review
